reason: Spec Memory MCP was not available in the detached lineage; canonical packet documents are the source of truth.

blockers.reason: Code Graph was unavailable; exact graphless fallback used.

focus: correctness: topology reproducibility and phase identity

findingDetails.title: Authoritative phase tree is not reproducible from its generator
findingDetails.evidence: Generator replay removes phase 005 child 004 and changes totals from 176/157 to 175/156.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add the executor child to TOP and enforce byte-for-byte no-diff regeneration.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct generator replay plus manifest, parent, and graph comparison.
findingDetails.claim: The checked-in authoritative phase tree cannot be regenerated from its declared source.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked parent and graph metadata for evidence that the fourth child was intentionally non-generated.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: A documented post-process might add the node, but none is present.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: A documented reproducible generation pipeline that preserves the node.

findingDetails.title: Root execution contract retains superseded phase identities
findingDetails.evidence: Current success and risk text routes through phases 014/015 and obsolete meanings for 007/009/011 despite a 000-011 map.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile every operational phase reference to the current map and label historical numbering explicitly.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact stale-number sweep compared with root map and graph metadata.
findingDetails.claim: Operational parent prose points executors at superseded phases.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Compared the current phase map, handoffs, and graph child list.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The numbers could be historical, but they are phrased as current success and mitigation steps.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Historical labels are explicit and all executable destinations name current phases.

noveltyJustification: First pass found two independent current-contract failures.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: The parent controls 176 generated phase nodes.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These jointly define executable topology.

searchLedger.invariant: The declared generator reproduces the authoritative manifest.

searchActions.queryOrPath: build-phase-tree.mjs to temporary output then cmp/diff
searchActions.result: Replay removed child 004 and changed totals.

searchLedger.rationale: Regeneration is destructive.

searchLedger.invariant: Operational prose names current executable phases.

searchActions.result: Current success and risk sections retain superseded destinations.

searchLedger.rationale: Executors can run the wrong phase.

focus: security: apply snapshot binding and unsafe operands

findingDetails.title: Rename apply is not bound to the reviewed repository and map snapshot
findingDetails.evidence: The plan records candidate/worktree and map identity, but apply has no requirement to revalidate those identities, cleanliness, sources, targets, and operation order immediately before the first write.
findingDetails.recommendation: Require an immutable plan identifier and atomic apply-time revalidation, with stale-plan and dirty-tree negative fixtures.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Engine spec/decision/checklist and harness searched for stale-plan, snapshot, HEAD, cleanliness, and revalidation contracts; the reference executor's blob CAS does not cover git mv.
findingDetails.claim: Explicit apply may execute a reviewed rename plan against changed repository or map state.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Audited the CAS reference executor and harness for an inherited rename-plan precondition.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The executor's blob CAS protects reference rewrites only; it does not bind path moves to the reviewed plan.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: A blocking apply-time identity and cleanliness revalidation contract with negative fixtures.

findingDetails.title: Declared leading-hyphen hazard lacks an executable rejection criterion
findingDetails.evidence: Policy explicitly identifies leading-hyphen CLI hazards, but engine acceptance and harness coverage enumerate leading/double underscores and collisions without requiring rejection or safe argv behavior for option-like paths.
findingDetails.recommendation: Define reject-or-safe-argv behavior and add leading-hyphen source/target fixtures that prove option-safe command construction.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Policy-to-engine-to-harness exact search for leading-hyphen, argv, option termination, and option-like operands.
findingDetails.claim: The plan names an option-like path hazard without a testable engine behavior.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched map validation, engine security checks, and harness negative scenarios for option-like path handling.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Generic unsafe-boundary rejection may be intended, but no acceptance criterion makes that behavior observable.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: An explicit reject-or-safe-argv rule plus a negative fixture.

noveltyJustification: Two independent mutation-boundary gaps not covered by correctness findings.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: The reviewed tooling controls repository-wide filesystem mutation.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These define the mutation trust boundary and its negative tests.

searchLedger.invariant: Apply executes exactly the reviewed plan against the reviewed repository and map state.

searchActions.result: Identity is recorded in the plan but not revalidated before git mv.

searchLedger.rationale: Review and mutation can target different snapshots.

searchLedger.invariant: Option-like path segments have explicit safe behavior.

searchActions.result: Hazard appears only in policy/context, not executable acceptance.

searchLedger.rationale: Command construction safety is unverified.

focus: traceability: packet identity and executable routing

findingDetails.title: Execution identity is split across packet 020, legacy 032, and branch slug 017
findingDetails.evidence: The canonical packet path and metadata are 020, while phase 000 allocates with 032-hyphen-naming but predicts branch/directory slug 017; phase 001 defers resolving the packet number until after phase 000.
findingDetails.recommendation: Choose one current identity before phase 000 and derive allocator input, expected branch/directory, titles, and parent labels from it.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact packet-number and worktree-grammar sweep across root, phase 000, phase 001, and child titles.
findingDetails.claim: The first executable phase cannot determine one canonical packet/worktree identity.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked canonical packet paths, graph IDs, and the allocator grammar for an explicit 032-to-020 alias.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: 032 may be historical design numbering, but phase 000 uses it as live allocator input and predicts 017 outputs.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: One pre-phase-000 identity decision and allocator contract generate matching packet, branch, and directory labels.

noveltyJustification: One new cross-consumer identity defect; semantic task/checklist coverage otherwise aligned.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Packet identity drives worktree allocation and all later evidence.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These surfaces create and propagate execution identity.

searchLedger.invariant: Packet, allocator input, branch, directory, and evidence use one identity.

searchActions.result: Live phase 000 contract combines all three identities.

searchLedger.rationale: The first phase can allocate and record the wrong worktree identity.

blockers.reason: Security-sensitive path and mutation contracts require two stabilization passes after full coverage.

focus: maintainability: metadata and standards-reference integrity

findingDetails.title: Most spec nodes point their HVR marker at a nonexistent path
findingDetails.evidence: 116 packet Markdown files reference .opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/hvr_rules.md, which does not exist; the live rule file is under sk-doc/shared/references and 17 packet files already use that path.
findingDetails.recommendation: Normalize HVR markers to the live shared reference or provide an intentional compatibility alias.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Corpus count excluding review artifacts plus filesystem existence check.

noveltyJustification: One advisory standards-pointer defect; metadata, descriptions, links, and placeholders otherwise clean.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: The packet contains 177 generated metadata/spec nodes.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These are the machine and human maintenance surfaces.

searchLedger.invariant: Every declared standards pointer resolves.

searchActions.result: 116 point to missing non-shared path; 17 point to live shared path.

searchLedger.rationale: Reviewers cannot follow the declared rule source.
